title: rubus angustisetus
intro: Rubus angustisetus, also known as the narrow-leaved bramble or small-leaved raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and wound healing. The herbal preparations of Rubus angustisetus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken as capsules, or applied topically to the skin.
